authoritative_sources.title: Regulation 5A: Operation of Interactive Gaming
authoritative_sources.publisher: Nevada Gaming Commission / Nevada Gaming Control Board
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Nevada Regulation 5A is a primary public operating rule for interactive gaming advertising, promotions, player registration, self-exclusion, suspicious-transaction handling, and records retention that constrains digital casino marketing operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Digital campaign eligibility review, promotion terms handling, and online channel recordkeeping
used_for: interactive gaming advertising and promotion boundaries

authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Internal Control Standards: Slots
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Nevada slot MICS are authoritative control standards for player-tracking accounts, promotional accounts, bonusing systems, documentation, segregation of duties, patron identification, rules display, and audit testing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Player-tracking setup, promotional account control, and campaign-close evidence review
used_for: approval and documentation thresholds for changes

authoritative_sources.title: Chapter 69C: Casino Control Commission and Division of Gaming Enforcement
authoritative_sources.publisher: New Jersey Division of Gaming Enforcement
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: New Jersey DGE Chapter 69C is a primary public rule source for casino advertising criteria, required responsible-gaming language, material-condition handling, promotional archive retention, and underage gaming boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Retail property advertising review, campaign archive retention, and underage or complimentary boundary handling
used_for: archive retention and underage patron boundary rules

authoritative_sources.title: Chapter 69G: Self-Exclusion
authoritative_sources.publisher: New Jersey Division of Gaming Enforcement
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: New Jersey DGE Chapter 69G is authoritative for self-exclusion procedures, targeted-mail and promotion suppression, player-club and complimentary restrictions, and third-party list-handling controls.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Audience qualification, suppression-file handling, and patron-protection stop conditions

authoritative_sources.title: Chapter 69O: Internet and Mobile Gaming
authoritative_sources.publisher: New Jersey Division of Gaming Enforcement
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: New Jersey DGE Chapter 69O is an authoritative public workflow reference for bonus and promotional wagering offers, account status messaging, manual adjustments, complaint handling, and online casino patron-eligibility controls.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Online casino bonus setup, active-offer records, and complaint or account exception handling

authoritative_sources.title: Advertising Standards
authoritative_sources.publisher: New Jersey Division of Gaming Enforcement
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The DGE advertising standards bulletin is an official workflow reference for review of bonus language, affiliate and digital placements, material conditions, and responsible-gaming messaging in gaming advertising operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Claim review, affiliate or channel launch readiness, and digital advertising exception handling

authoritative_sources.title: Responsible Gaming Code of Conduct
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The AGA code is the recognized U.S. casino industry standard for responsible gaming advertising, legal-age controls, digital channel handling, opt-out and privacy expectations, third-party marketing governance, and internal review discipline.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Responsible marketing controls, digital communication settings, and internal review governance
used_for: third-party marketing governance and review process

workflow_stages.name: Casino campaign intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm property, jurisdiction, channel, approved campaign brief, offer matrix, approval state, and current systems of record before casino marketing work progresses.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Audience qualification and offer-setup validation
workflow_stages.description: Use player-tracking, loyalty, suppression, self-exclusion, and patron-account records to determine whether the approved audience and promotional coding are safe to activate.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Proofing, channel launch readiness, and live exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Use approved terms, responsible-gaming messaging, creative proofs, vendor handoff files, and digital or direct-mail launch evidence to decide whether work can launch, hold, rework, or escalate.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Fulfillment, redemption, and patron-impact exception management
workflow_stages.description: Track in-flight offer delivery, bounced or suppressed sends, kiosk or bonusing activation issues, patron complaints, and redemption mismatches while preserving compliance and customer-impact boundaries.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence, archive retention, and authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Only close work when launch, fulfillment, archive, suppression, and exception evidence exists and any stricter tenant, property, or jurisdiction rule has been applied.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: approved campaign briefs, promotional calendars, launch tickets, and owner maps
offer matrices, terms and conditions, disclaimers, responsible-gaming messages, and approval records
player-tracking segments, loyalty tiers, promotional codes, free-play or bonusing setup records, and player-club restrictions
self-exclusion and exclusion lists, communication preference data, suppression files, and patron eligibility logs
creative proofs, seed-list evidence, archive captures, vendor handoff files, send logs, and launch timestamps
redemption summaries, kiosk or bonusing reports, patron complaint records, and exception tickets
systems_of_record: offer engine, bonusing, kiosk, or free-play management system
digital channel platforms for email, SMS, web, app, or direct-mail fulfillment

allowed: Assess whether documented casino marketing records support advancing, holding, reworking, or completing a campaign, offer, or launch queue item.
Prepare evidence-backed execution updates, completion records, exception notes, and escalation packages tied to player-tracking, suppression, offer, and archive records.
Request missing prerequisites and record-of-truth evidence before changing lifecycle state.
escalate: Self-exclusion, underage, patron-account, responsible-gaming, or privacy signals change the answer.
Offer terms, disclosures, public regulation, tenant SOPs, or vendor instructions conflict materially.
Creative approval, budget authority, legal interpretation, compliance signoff, or promotional liability approval is needed to resolve the item.
Required player-tracking, suppression, archive, or fulfillment evidence is missing and would change launch or completion.
refuse: Requests to launch gaming advertising without approved material conditions, disclosures, and responsible-gaming handling
Requests to alter offer value, patron eligibility, or player exceptions without authoritative approval records
Requests to target self-excluded, suspended, restricted, or underage patrons
Requests that treat casino marketing operations as generic hospitality marketing without gaming-specific records

domain_failure_modes: launching a gaming campaign without approved material conditions, responsible-gaming language, or archive evidence
sending or activating offers for self-excluded, suspended, restricted, or underage patrons because suppression controls were skipped or treated as optional
treating player-tracking, CRM, bonusing, and kiosk mismatches as ordinary marketing variance instead of blocking launch or fulfillment
closing campaign work without proof of launch, fulfillment, redemption, archive retention, and exception resolution
flattening casino marketing operations into generic property marketing language that omits player-club, offer-code, self-exclusion, and promotional-account controls
drifting into legal, compliance, strategy, creative approval, budget, or patron-exception authority

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific promotional disclosure, archive, and channel rules
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: State and tribal gaming regimes can narrow what disclosures, delivery channels, retention periods, and patron-eligibility gates apply to a promotion.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require runtime retrieval of property, jurisdiction, and channel-specific policy before final launch, suppression, or completion decisions.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific delegation for offer-value changes, host exceptions, and vendor launch approvals
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Public baselines define control boundaries, but tenant governance decides which promotional changes or campaign exceptions casino marketing operations may execute directly.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Retrieve the tenant authority matrix and keep outputs recommendation-only whenever approval ownership is unresolved.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because the remaining ambiguity is explicitly constrained to jurisdiction and tenant retrieval at runtime.
The research output constrains the lane to casino marketing execution-state management with explicit player-tracking, suppression, disclosure, and responsible-gaming discipline.
